name: Toad-in-The-Hole (Yorkshire Pudding W/Sausage
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor fitted with the steel blade or in a blender, blend flour, milk, eggs, salt, and pepper for 5 seconds.
2. Turn off the motor and scrape down the sides of the container with a rubber spatula.
3. Blend the batter for an additional 20 seconds.
4. Let the batter stand, covered with plastic wrap, for one hour.
5. In a skillet, combine sausages pierced lightly with a needle and water. Bring to a boil over moderately high heat and boil the sausages until the water is evaporated.
6. Sauté the sausages for 7 minutes or until golden brown, adding oil if necessary to prevent sticking.
7. Transfer the sausages to a plate. Pour 3 tbsp of fat from the skillet into an 8-inch square baking pan and heat in a preheated very hot oven (450°F) for two minutes.
8. Stir in the batter, pour one third of it into the pan, and bake for two minutes until set.
9. Arrange the sausages on top of the batter and pour the remaining batter over them.
10. Bake the mixture for 15 minutes at a very hot temperature (450°F), then reduce to moderate heat (375°F) and continue baking for 10-15 minutes or until puffed and golden brown.
11. Serve immediately with Dijon-style mustard, if desired.
